book.title_en: A Glass Bridge Between Two Worlds
book.title_ja: ビーズの魔法：ムラノと京都をつなぐガラスの橋
book.author: Maurizio Lotter

system_principle: The system is a bilateral craft field sustained by a single operator (Lotter) and a single object (the bead). Murano and Kyoto do not merge: they remain co-active regimes of perception held in tension.

primary_loops.label: furnace_to_form
primary_loops.propagation_logic: glass transforms once; the bead is the residue of irreversible thermal decision
primary_loops.description: Molten glass → decision → fixed form. The irreversibility of glass transformation mirrors the irreversibility of cultural encounter.

primary_loops.label: murano_kyoto_dual_practice
primary_loops.propagation_logic: one body sustains two craft regimes without translation or synthesis
primary_loops.description: Lotter as single operator who makes the bridge possible: thirty-plus years of practice in two traditions simultaneously active.

primary_loops.label: gift_delay_revelation
primary_loops.propagation_logic: meaning emerges only after spatial and temporal distance from origin
primary_loops.description: Venice → pocket → Como → examination → embodiment. The field of the bead requires delay to become fully visible.

primary_loops.label: embodied_heritage_chain
primary_loops.propagation_logic: craft exists only as continuous bodily transmission across generations
primary_loops.description: Franchini → Murano tradition → Lotter → Kyoto. A chain that can break at any point — the fragile loop in the system.

primary_loops.label: bilingual_irreducibility
primary_loops.propagation_logic: EN/JA coexist as non-translational parallel fields producing a third irreducible space
primary_loops.description: The universal language of beauty: shared epistemic limit rather than shared language.

global_state.system_behavior: the bead functions as a non-resolvable attractor that maintains Murano and Kyoto as co-active but non-fusing systems

catalog_connection.luna_piena_sulla_citta_antica: bilingual mediation as structural constraint (EN/JA vs IT/JA)
